en.extract:
The black-bellied starling (Notopholia corusca) is a species of starling in the family Sturnidae, originally described by zoologist Alexander von Nordmann in 1835. It is found in Eswatini, Kenya, Mozambique, Somalia, South Africa, Tanzania, and Zimbabwe.
Their iris color changes by mood or behavior due to fluctuations in blood flow rates.

The '''black-bellied starling''' ('''''Notopholia corusca''''') is a species of [[starling]] in the family [[Sturnidae]], originally described by [[zoologist]] [[Alexander von Nordmann]] in 1835. It is found in Eswatini, Kenya, Mozambique, Somalia, South Africa, Tanzania, and Zimbabwe.

Their iris color changes by mood or behavior due to fluctuations in blood flow rates.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=McCulloch |first1=D. |year=1963 |title=Colour change in the iris of the Black-bellied starling ''Lamprotornis corruscus'' |journal=Ostrich |volume=34 |page=177}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Britton |first1=P.L. |last2=Britton |first2=H. |year=1970 |title=Eye colour of the black-bellied glossy starling |journal=J. East Afr. Nat. Hist. Soc. Bull. |volume=1970 |page=46}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Chittenden |first1=H. |last2=Myburgh |first2=N. |year=1994 |title=Eye colour change in Black-bellied starlings |journal=Birding South. Afr. |volume=46 |page=117}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Craig |first1=Adrian |title=Black-bellied Starling (Notopholia corusca) |date=2020-03-04 |journal=Birds of the World |editor-last=Billerman |editor-first=Shawn M. |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/bbgsta1/1.0/introduction |access-date=2024-06-03 |publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.bbgsta1.01 |last2=Feare |first2=C. J. |editor2-last=Keeney |editor2-first=Brooke K. |editor3-last=Rodewald |editor3-first=Paul G. |editor4-last=Schulenberg |editor4-first=Thomas S.|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
